title: Pgbus — Health
description: Backlog, dead-letter activity, dead-tuple growth, and MVCC horizon. The 'should I page someone?' dashboard.

graphs.title: Queue depth (visible vs total)
graphs.description: Visible depth excludes messages whose VT hasn't expired. A divergence between the two means workers are slow but the queue isn't growing.

graphs.title: Oldest message age (seconds)
graphs.description: Per-queue head-of-line waiting time. If this climbs while queue depth stays flat, a single poison message is stuck in the VT loop.

graphs.title: DLQ depth + failed events
graphs.description: Messages that exceeded max_retries plus the failed-events table. Spikes after a deploy point at a regression.

graphs.title: Active processes
graphs.description: Workers + dispatcher + scheduler currently heartbeating into pgbus_processes.

graphs.title: Dead tuples in queue/archive tables
graphs.description: If autovacuum can't keep up the index gets bloated and lock acquisition slows. Tune autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor on the offending tables when this climbs.

graphs.title: Oldest open transaction (seconds)
graphs.description: MVCC horizon pin. Long-running transactions prevent VACUUM from cleaning the dead tuples above. Anything over 60s is a smell.

graphs.title: Worker recycles per minute
graphs.description: Recycles by reason. Steady max_jobs is healthy; spiking max_memory means you have a leak.
